prompt:
Ticket: log follower for the metrics shipper agent.

The shipper needs to tail service log files the way tail -F does, except as a library we drive from our own poll loop — no inotify, no goroutines, no background anything. Package logtail at the root of module go-logtail, stdlib only.

API:
- New(path string) *Follower — path may not exist yet; that's normal at boot.
- Resume(path string, st State) *Follower — pick up where a previous follower checkpointed.
- (*Follower).Poll() ([]string, error) — deliver every line that has become available since the last poll, in order, exactly once. Lines come back without their trailing newline; a trailing \r goes too (some services log CRLF). A missing file is not an error — return no lines and keep waiting. Real I/O problems are errors.
- (*Follower).State() State — State{Offset int64}, the byte offset consumed in the current file. Only complete lines count: bytes of a not-yet-terminated final line are NOT consumed, so a checkpoint taken between polls never loses or duplicates a line. We persist this between shipper restarts.
- (*Follower).Close() error — safe even if no file was ever opened.

Rotation is the whole point, and it must work purely from what Poll can observe:
- rename + recreate (classic logrotate): drain what's left of the old file first — including a final unterminated line, which is delivered as-is since it can never be completed — then start the new file from byte 0. If the replacement doesn't exist yet, drain the old one and wait.
- copytruncate / file rewritten shorter in place: detect the shrink, reset to offset 0, read the new content.
- A resumed offset beyond the current file size means the file was replaced while we were down: start from 0.

Acceptance tests: tail_test.go (protected). They script appends, renames, truncations and restarts in a temp dir and call Poll after each step — pull-based means all of this is deterministic. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... must pass.
